title: INDEX prevents JOIN from discovering matches
poc: CREATE TABLE TEST1 ( one VARCHAR(10) , two INT ) ;
CREATE TABLE TEST2 ( one VARCHAR(10) , two INT ) ;
SELECT count(*) FROM ( SELECT one , two FROM TEST1 ) AS a INNER JOIN ( SELECT one , two FROM TEST2 ) AS B on A.one = B.one AND A.two = B.two ;
CREATE INDEX onedex ON TEST1 ( one , two ) ;

SELECT count(*) FROM ( SELECT one , two FROM TEST1 ) AS a INNER JOIN ( SELEC
 T one , two FROM TEST2 ) AS B on A.one = B.one AND A.two = B.two ;
key_bug_features: INDEX on TEST1 disrupts JOIN match discovery
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE TEST1 ( one VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL, two INT DEFAULT 0, three DATE );
CREATE TABLE TEST2 ( one VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL, two INT CHECK (two >= 0), four FLOAT );
CREATE INDEX idx_test1 ON TEST1 ( one, two );
